title: cyanus cheiranthifolius subsp. willdenowii
intro: Cyanus cheiranthifolius subsp. willdenowii, also known as the Willdenow's cornflower, is a subspecies of the cornflower plant in the aster family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Willdenow's cornflower include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for use in herbal remedies, showcasing its versatility in traditional herbal medicine.
